design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling jungle levels by collecting gems, jumping/climbing, avoiding or defeating enemies with projectile weapons, then locating a specific character or boss to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure (six-minute level limit) combined with gem collection quota and combat/navigation platforming
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody Mowgli escaping the jungle and Shere Khan's pursuit, meeting iconic characters from the film while demonstrating survival skills through combat and traversal
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: SNES version diverges significantly from other ports by emphasizing exploration and character-finding (Baloo/exit) over strict gem collection, creating a more narrative-driven progression structure tied to meeting plot characters rather than pure resource grinding

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulty tiers that scale gem collection requirements (8 gems on easy, 10 on medium, 12 on hard from 15 total available), allowing player choice in completion threshold

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon/power-up pickup system: starts with banana projectile, can collect invincibility masks, coconuts, double banana shots, and boomerang bananas during levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Chapter-based structure where each stage is part of sequential chapters that advance the plot; must collect sufficient gems then locate chapter-specific character (friendly NPC) or defeat boss to progress
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select cheat code enables direct access to any level
Multiple difficulty settings alter gem collection requirements
Scoring system based on gems and item collection encourages optimization
Different weapon/power-up placements encourage varied traversal strategies

world_building_techniques: Chapter introductions describing story context and stage objectives
Character encounters embedded in level completion (meeting Bagheera, Baloo, King Louie, Kaa as progression gates)
Film-based soundtrack featuring 'The Bare Necessities,' 'I Wan'na Be Like You,' and 'Colonel Hathi's March' to reinforce Disney narrative
Boss encounters every other level tied to plot (ultimate confrontation with Shere Khan)

boss_design.philosophy: Boss characters serve as chapter climaxes in the narrative progression; require projectile-based damage (jumping on bosses is ineffective), creating distinct combat mechanics from regular platforming
notable_bosses: Shere Khan (final boss, represents climactic threat from plot)

faction_indicators: Mowgli's journey from wolf pack to human village represents cultural/faction transition
Meeting of jungle allies (Bagheera, Baloo) vs. antagonists (Shere Khan, Kaa) creates opposing force dynamics
danger_signals: Shere Khan as persistent hunting threat throughout narrative
Hypnotist snake Kaa represents supernatural danger
Jungle wildlife enemies indicate environmental hostility
environmental_storytelling: Jungle traversal via vine-swinging, climbing, and platforming conveys primal wilderness setting
Character placement in levels suggests they inhabit specific jungle territories
Level progression from deep jungle toward human village endpoint mirrors narrative arc

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (SNES version-specific mechanics, level structure, cheat codes)
wikipedia - The Jungle Book video game (gameplay mechanics, plot, character list, development, reception)
